currentTask.name: Cognitive Surrender mitigation: CATFISH protocol + Recognition model + Decision scaffold + Triple Debt tracking
currentTask.goal: Build three anti-cognitive-surrender subsystems based on research findings: CATFISH adversarial planning dissent, Recognition enforced-participation gate, unified Decision scaffold, and Triple Debt measurement

environment.note: Configure per-workspace in your agent runtime config (e.g., OpenCode at `$HOME/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc`, Claude Code at `.claude/settings.json`).
environment.topicFoldersPath: Change to your topic folders parent

whatChanged: Research: cognitive-surrender.md expanded from 383 to 766 lines with 40 sources, 6 agent-mitigation architecture families
CATFISH Protocol: scripts/plan-challenge.sh dynamic dissent engine + plan-guard.sh --challenge flag
Recognition Model: scripts/comprehension-gate.sh enforced participation + skill-toolset.py audit trail
Decision Scaffold: scripts/decision.sh DCI decision packet with evaluate/log/review/audit
Triple Debt: scripts/triple-debt.sh tracking technical/cognitive/intent debt with history
Research Sufficiency: scripts/research-sufficiency.sh 6-phase methodology + 7 red flag checks
Quality vs Speed: scripts/quality-speed-gate.sh heuristic test depth recommendation
Unified Phase Gate: extended phase-gate.sh --check-quality delegates to all anti-surrender checks
Post-Failure Decision: error-counter.sh decide/classify subcommands (C/S/E failure classification)
Commit signal: session-boundary.sh now outputs commit suggestion after verified phases
commands/*.md: updated all workflow commands to reference unified phase gate
Deep repo analysis: 16 comprehension gaps, 27 decision mechanisms mapped, 10 unsolved types
filesTouched: commands/implement.md (+31 lines, decision point + unified gate)
verification: All 68 smoke tests pass (0 regressions, up from 59)
decision.sh tested with low/medium/high stakes + manual log + review + audit
error-counter.sh decide/classify tested end-to-end with real failure context
All JSON logs validate (no malformed entries)
Quality gate passes on all commits (non-ASCII normalized)
Branch management: feat/recognition-participation merged, s82-decision-workflow merged and deleted
keyLearnings: The repo has 16 read-to-act paths with zero comprehension enforcement. The most sophisticated workflow docs are entirely advisory --- the system diagnoses surrender but prescribes only self-medication.

A permanent devil's advocate makes things WORSE (adversarial fatigue). CATFISH dynamic injection (23% better) requires architectural heterogeneity --- subagent dispatch with fresh context and counterfactual framing.

The Recognition model principle: collapse is driven by interaction architecture, not model capability. 'Any system that does not enforce participation will train its users not to think.' Applied to agents this means: the system must require structured extraction before acting.

The Triple Debt model (Storey): AI makes cognitive debt and intent debt grow faster than technical debt. Code gets written but nobody understands why. The fix: measure all three explicitly at task boundaries.
DCI decision packet pattern (selected option + residual objections + reopen conditions) provides the audit trail for decision-making that prevents premature convergence.
Minimal design is a quality GATE, not a design CONSTRAINT. Systemic analysis first, then minimal as final optimization pass.
residualRisk: plan-challenge.sh, comprehension-gate.sh, decision.sh, and triple-debt.sh are committed to main but not yet used in production agent workflow. The quality-gate integration is advisory (WARN not ERROR) for most checks except CATFISH blocking findings. Decision workflow integration in commands/*.md is documented but agent-mediated. The 10 unsolved decision types (Path D) have 3 of 10 resolved (research sufficiency, quality/speed, post-failure). Remaining 7 (model selection, file-level edit, escalation routing, plan confidence, risk-adjusted autonomy, commit-vs-checkpoint) are ready for new session forks.
immediateNextSteps: New session 1: model-selection tool (model registry + task classifier)
New session 2: file-level edit decision tool
Run triple-debt.sh assess at task boundaries to start tracking cognitive drift
No push needed --- origin/main is at latest commit 434b584
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
Question Gate is automatic: vague requests trigger the Clarification Protocol before implementation
CATFISH workflow: plan-guard.sh --challenge then plan-challenge.sh prompt/reconcile for subagent dissent
Recognition workflow: comprehension-gate.sh extract before implement, verify via quality-gate
Decision workflow: decision.sh evaluate/log/review/audit for structured decision-making
Triple Debt: triple-debt.sh assess at task boundaries; integrate with retrospect via --debt
Unified gate: phase-gate.sh <phase> --check-quality for all pre-phase checks
Post-failure: error-counter.sh increment <op> then decide <op> then classify <op> <C|S|E>
doNotRepeat: 2026-05-13: Researched cognitive surrender using only webfetch --- blind to Google results. Use websearch first.

events.summary: Session 83: Cognitive surrender mitigation --- CATFISH protocol (plan-challenge.sh), Recognition model (comprehension-gate.sh), Decision scaffold (decision.sh), Triple Debt tracking (triple-debt.sh), Research sufficiency (research-sufficiency.sh), Quality-speed gate (quality-speed-gate.sh), Unified phase gate (phase-gate.sh --check-quality), Post-failure decision (error-counter.sh decide/classify). 12 commits, 8 new scripts, 68 tests (up from 59). Remote synced.
